item.title: Garda / Assertio Deal (Rolvedon)
item.description:
• Summary: Garda has acquired Assertio and the chemotherapy-induced neutropenia treatment Rolvedon (eflapegrastim) in a $125 million deal, strengthening its oncology supportive care portfolio.
  • MoAs: Rolvedon is a long-acting granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF) analog that binds to G-CSF receptors on neutrophil precursors, stimulating production and maturation to prevent infections during myelosuppressive chemotherapy.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Roche / C4 Therapeutics Partnership
item.description:
• Summary: Roche and C4 Therapeutics have expanded their existing partnership, with Roche paying $20 million upfront and committing up to $1 billion in milestones for access to molecular glue protein degraders. This collaboration aims to advance novel oncology therapies leveraging C4 Therapeutics' degrader platform.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: AI Biotech Ailux
item.description:
• Summary: China-based AI biotech Ailux has hired a Chief Scientific Officer and is targeting entry into clinical trials by 2027. The company is leveraging artificial intelligence to accelerate drug discovery and development.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: PhRMA Lobbyist Steve Ubl Exit
item.description:
• Summary: Steve Ubl, the long-standing leader of the pharmaceutical lobbying group PhRMA, is stepping down by year-end. His exit comes amid ongoing industry discussions on topics such as genetics, GLP-1 drugs, AbbVie, and the 340B drug pricing program.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, BioSpace

item.title: Innovent CDMO Expansion
item.description:
• Summary: Innovent's contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) unit has received regulatory approval to produce biologics at a large-scale plant in China. This expansion enhances Innovent's capacity to support global biopharmaceutical manufacturing.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace

item.title: Gilead / Kymera Partnership (Protein Degrader)
item.description:
• Summary: Gilead Sciences has partnered with Kymera Therapeutics, paying $45 million upfront for a preclinical protein degrader to enhance Gilead's oncology pipeline. The collaboration focuses on developing targeted protein degradation therapies for cancer.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech
